rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component implementation annexes for implementation-level signal routing bindings.
Signal routing assignments in AGREE should reflect the exact port connections defined in the AADL implementation.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture scope when inserting AGREE annexes.
Use component implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings.
Avoid unsafe Boolean data range comparisons in AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in AGREE must reference valid ports and signals in the implementation scope.
anti_patterns: Do not insert AGREE contracts into component type annexes for implementation-specific signal routing.
Do not perform numeric comparisons on opaque Boolean or enumeration data types in AGREE.
irrelevant_topics: AADL structural extraction for component types and features
